title: Area code 712

infobox.value: is the telephone [area code](/wiki/Area_code "Area code") in the [North American Numbering Plan](/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan "North American Numbering Plan") (NANP) for the most western part of [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"), including the cities of [Spencer](/wiki/Spencer,_Iowa "Spencer, Iowa"), [Le Mars](/wiki/Le_Mars,_Iowa "Le Mars, Iowa"), [Sioux City](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"), [Council Bluffs](/wiki/Council_Bluffs,_Iowa "Council Bluffs, Iowa"), [Red Oak](/wiki/Red_Oak,_Iowa "Red Oak, Iowa"), [Sheldon](/wiki/Sheldon,_Iowa "Sheldon, Iowa"), [Storm Lake](/wiki/Storm_Lake,_Iowa "Storm Lake, Iowa"), [Carroll](/wiki/Carroll,_Iowa "Carroll, Iowa"), [Sac City](/wiki/Sac_City,_Iowa "Sac City, Iowa"), [Logan](/wiki/Logan,_Iowa "Logan, Iowa"), and [Shenandoah](/wiki/Shenandoah,_Iowa "Shenandoah, Iowa").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Telephone area code for western Iowa, United States

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/56/Area_code_712.svg/330px-Area_code_712.svg.png)

Numbering plan areas of [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa") with the red area indicating area code 712

**Area code 712** is the telephone [area code](/wiki/Area_code "Area code") in the [North American Numbering Plan](/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan "North American Numbering Plan") (NANP) for the most western part of [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"), including the cities of [Spencer](/wiki/Spencer,_Iowa "Spencer, Iowa"), [Le Mars](/wiki/Le_Mars,_Iowa "Le Mars, Iowa"), [Sioux City](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"), [Council Bluffs](/wiki/Council_Bluffs,_Iowa "Council Bluffs, Iowa"), [Red Oak](/wiki/Red_Oak,_Iowa "Red Oak, Iowa"), [Sheldon](/wiki/Sheldon,_Iowa "Sheldon, Iowa"), [Storm Lake](/wiki/Storm_Lake,_Iowa "Storm Lake, Iowa"), [Carroll](/wiki/Carroll,_Iowa "Carroll, Iowa"), [Sac City](/wiki/Sac_City,_Iowa "Sac City, Iowa"), [Logan](/wiki/Logan,_Iowa "Logan, Iowa"), and [Shenandoah](/wiki/Shenandoah,_Iowa "Shenandoah, Iowa").

Area code 712 was one of the [original three area codes](/wiki/Original_NANP_area_codes "Original NANP area codes") for the state of Iowa established in 1947. It is the only original area code in Iowa that still has its original boundaries, and one of the few remaining original area codes nationwide (not counting those covering an entire state) that has never been split or [overlaid](/wiki/Overlay_plan "Overlay plan"). Despite the proliferation of cell phones and pagers (particularly in Sioux City and Council Bluffs), it is projected to stay that way until at least 2031.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
